title: agrimonia eupatoria subvar. rotundifolia
intro: Agrimonia eupatoria subvar. rotundifolia, also known as round-leaved agrimony, is a subvariety of the agrimony plant, a flowering herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of round-leaved agrimony include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Round-leaved agrimony is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be found in some commercial skincare products due to its antioxidant and anti-aging properties.
